Facts.text: The future Zeno I is named associate emperor by Emperor Leo I
Battle of Mizushima: Japanese Taira clan defeat the Minamoto
680 Jews are arrested in England for counterfeiting coins, and 293 are hanged
(O.S.) John Balliol becomes King of Scots
England, Spain and the Holy Roman Empire sign anti-French covenant The Treaty of Westminster
Brazilian general Henrique Dias wins a decisive battle against the Dutch in Brazil
Peace of the Pyrenees is signed between France and Spain
Printer John Zenger, arrested for libel against NY colonial governor William Cosby; later acquitted
First City Troop Philadelphia City Cavalry formed at Carpenter's Hall, Philadelphia, one of the oldest US military units still in service
Church of England organizes in New England
Beginning of a 5 day New England Blizzard that covers thousands of houses and kills hundreds
Congress meets for the first time in the newly built but still incomplete Capitol Building in Washington, D.C., established by the Residence Act of 1790
Napoleonic Wars: Battle of Krasnoi duringNapoleon's retreat from Moscow
The Delta Phi fraternity, America's oldest continuous social fraternity, founded at Union College in Schenectady, New York
Ecuador and Venezuela separate from Gran Colombia
Fugitive slave George Latimer captured in Boston
Opera "Linda di Chamounix" by Gaetano Donizetti is produced in London
Street signs authorized at San Francisco intersections
On the Sonoita River in present-day southern Arizona, the United States Army establishes Fort Buchanan in order to help control new land acquired in the Gadsden Purchase.
Origin of Modified Julian Period
Confederate Secretary of War George B. Randolph resigns
-Dec 4th) Battle of Knoxville, Tennessee
Opera "Mignon" by Ambroise Thomas premieres at the Opéra-Comique in Paris, Francce
Englishman James Moore wins 1st bicycle race, racing 13K Paris to Rouen
Suez Canal in Egypt opens, linking the Mediterranean and Red seas
National Rifle Association is first chartered in the State of New York
Rival cities of Buda & Pest unite to form the capital of Hungary
Emigrant ship Cospatrick catches fire & sinks off Auckland, New Zealand
American Theosophical Society founded by Mme Blavatsky & Colonel Olcott
Russia launches a surprise night attack that overruns Turkish forces at Kars, Armenia
First assassination attempt against King Umberto I of Italy
British gunboat HMS Flirt fires at and destroys village of Torofani on the Forcado River in Niger, in retaliation for attack on British owned factory that left 5 Brits missing
The Serbian Army, with Russian support, invades Bulgaria
Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky' conducts the premiere of his 5th Symphony at the Mariinsky Theatre in St Petersburg, Russia
Union Pacific begins daily through service, Chicago-Portland & San Francisco
Daily Racing Form founded
Dahomey (Benin) becomes a French protectorate
In the Treaty of Petropolis, Bolivia cedes the territory of Arce to Brazil; Bolivia gains rail and water outlets in the east
The Eulsa Treaty is signed between Japan and Korea
1st US dental hygienists course forms, Bridgeport, Connecticut
The first ship sails through the Panama Canal, connecting the Atlantic and Pacific oceans
US declares Panama Canal Zone neutral
German troops evacuate Brussels
Social Democratic Party becomes Communistc Party Holland: CPH
NHL's Chicago Black Hawks play their 1st game, beat Tor St Pats 4-1
Tornado hits Washington, D.C.
Boston Garden officially opens
Notre Dame finally loses a football game at home after 23 years
Pascual Ortiz Rubio elected president of Mexico
Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney opens Whitney Museum of American Art on West Eighth Street. NY, after the Met Museum refuses to accept her collection
German government of von Papen resigns
Marx Brothers film "Duck Soup," directed by Leo McCarey and starring the Marx Brothers, is released in the US
United States recognizes Soviet Union, opens trade
Edgar Bergen and dummy Charlie McCarthy become an overnight success on radio
Britain's Lord Halifax visits Germany, beginning of appeasement
Italy passes its own version of anti-Jewish Nuremberg laws
German U-boat torpedoes passenger ship
The Rome-Rio de Janeiro air connection created
Green Bay Packers become 1st NFL team to travel by plane
Virgil Thomson's 2nd Symphony, premieres
New world air speed record 606 mph (975 kph) set by HJ Wilson of RAF
The Screen Actors Guild in the US implements an anti-Communist loyalty oath
Britain's House of Commons votes to nationalize steel industry
Britain reports development of the world's first nuclear-powered heating system
St Louis Browns officially become the Baltimore Baseball Club Inc
The remaining human inhabitants of the Blasket Islands, Kerry, Ireland are evacuated to the mainland
United States joins the United Nations in condemning Israel's raid on Jordan on October 4, 1953
USSR performs atmospheric nuclear test
WBOY TV channel 12 in Clarksburg, West Virginia (NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
KAII TV channel 7 in Wailuku, HI (NBC) begins broadcasting
De Beers firm of South Africa announces synthetic diamond
William Shea shows proposed NYC stadium with transparent roof
New MLB Washington franchise is awarded to Elwood Quesada
USSR performs nuclear test at Eastern Kazakh, Semipalitinsk USSR
British Labour Party installs weapon embargo against South Africa
General Meeting of UN refuses admittance of People's Republic of China
The NVA ambushes American troops of the 7th Cavalry at Landing Zone Albany in the la Drang Valley, almost wiping them out
William Eckert is unanimously elected commissioner of baseball
Leonids meteor shower peaks (150,000+ per hour)
Beatles Ltd and Apple Music Ltd swap names
French author Régis Debray sentenced to 30 years in Bolivia
Surveyor 6 becomes 1st man-made object to lift off Moon
"Heidi Game", NBC cuts to show "Heidi" and misses Raider's rally to beat Jets, 43-32
Alexandros Panagoulis is condemned to death for attempting to assassinate Greek dictator George Papadopoulos
KHNE TV channel 29 in Hastings, NB (PBS) begins broadcasting
SALT-discussions open in Helsinki Finland
British newspaper Sun puts 1st pinup girl on page 3 (Stephanie Rahn)
Russia lands Lunokhod 1 unmanned remote-controlled vehicle on Moon
Greek regime attacks students with tanks, 100s killed
Aliança Operário-Camponesa (Worker-Peasant Alliance) founded in Portugal as a front of PCP(m-l).
Bonnie Bryant becomes the only left-handed player in history to win an LPGA Tour event; scores a 3-stroke victory in the Bill Branch Golf Classic in Fort Myers, Florida
Union of Banana Exporting Countries (UPEB) forms
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
Bernard Pomerance's "Elephant Man" premieres in London
Daniel Okrent sketches out 1st draft rules for Rotisserie Baseball on a flight to Austin, TX
NY Stars (WBL) home opener at Madison Square Garden in NYC
WHHM Television in Washington, D.C., becomes the first African-American public-broadcasting television station
NBA NY Knick Bill Cartwright, ties record of 19 of 19 free throws
Harm Wiersma retains checkers world championship
Philadelphia Flyers win 13th straight NHL game
Golden State Warrior Purvis Short scores career high 59 points though team loses to NJ Nets 124-110
Islanders score 20 assists against Rangers
NBC's premiere of controversial TV thriller "Hostage Flight"
NY Jets best offensive production beating Tampa Bay 62-28
George Bell is 1st Blue Jay ever to win the AL MVP
Bret Saberhagensigns record $2,966,667 per year KC Royal contract
Student demonstration in Prague put down by riot police, leading to an uprising (the Velvet Revolution) that will topple the communist government on Dec. 29
American singer David Crosby breaks his left leg, ankle and shoulder in a motorcycle accident in Los Angeles, California
1st TV condom ad aired (FOX- TV)
Detroit Lion Mike Utley is paralized in a game vs LA Rams
"The Bodyguard" movie soundtrack is released by Arista Records (Billboard Album of the Year 1993)
Blue Jay Nigel Wilson is 1st pick of Marlins in expansion draft
Dateline NBC airs a demonstration show General Motors trucks, blowing up on impact, later revealed NBC rigged test
Erling Kagge begins successful exploration at South pole
Antonov AN-124 flies in South Iran against mountain: 17 killed
US House of Representatives approve Nafta
3rd Germany government of Kohl forms
Irish government of Reynolds resigns
"Present Laughter" opens at Walter Kerr Theater NYC
Time White and T. Assebework discover the first fossils of 2.5 million year old Australopithecus garhi, an ancestor on the human family tree, in Bouri, Middle Awash, Ethiopia
In Luxor, Egypt, 62 people are killed by 6 Islamic militants outside the Temple of Hatshepsut, known as Luxor massacre (The police then kill the assailants).
Tori Amos release a compilation of her videos, "Tori Amos: The Complete Collection: 1992-1998"
A catastrophic landslide in Log pod Mangartom, Slovenia, kills 7, and causes millions of SIT of damage. It is one of the worst catastrophes in Slovenia in the past 100 years.
Kmart Corp. announces it is buying Sears, Roebuck and Co. for $11 billion USD and naming the newly merged company Sears Holdings Corporation.
Italy's choice of national anthem, "Il Canto degli Italiani", becomes official in law for the first time, almost 60 years after it was provisionally chosen following the birth of the republic.
Official naming of element 111, Roentgenium (Rg).
Japan, the world's second-biggest economy, slides into its first recession in seven years
50 schoolchildren and a bus driver are killed after a train collides with a school bus in Manfalut, Egypt
50 people are killed after a Boeing 737 aircraft crashes in Kazan, Russia
Abdulla Yameen becomes the President of the Maldives
Giorgi Margvelashvili becomes the President of Georgia
The Church of England adopts legislation enabling the appointment of female bishops
Ireland's 1st same-sex wedding takes place - Cormac Gollogly and Richard Dowling marry in Clonmel, County Tipperary
Suicide bomber kills more than 30 in a market in Yola, north-eastern Nigeria, with Boko Haram blamed
Smaller pyramid find within 2 known Kukulkan "nesting" pyramids announced at Chichen Itza in Yucatan, Mexico, dating to 550-80 AD
Ireland beats Rugby World Cup champions New Zealand, 16-9 at Aviva Stadium, Dublin; first victory over the All Blacks ever on home soil; flyhalf Johnny Sexton kicks 3 penalties and a conversion
Missing Argentine naval submarine ARA San Juan with 44 on board found on sea floor a year after it disappeared off coast of Argentina
Protests across France against rising fuel prices leave 400 injured
British Columbia declares a state of emergency after an "atmospheric river" storm causes widespread rain and flooding
Delhi authorities order schools shut till further notice and construction halted as Supreme Court calls for a "pollution lockdown" as city battles winter smog
Garbiñe Muguruza becomes first Spanish player to win WTA Finals defeating Estonian Anett Kontaveit 6–3, 7–5 in Zapopan, Mexico
Record number of Americans, over 100,000, died of drug overdoses April 2020 - April 2021 according to the CDC
US capitol rioter QAnon Shaman [Jacob Chansley] sentenced to three years in prison by a federal court
US National Book Award given to Jason Mott (fiction), Tiya Miles (nonfiction) and Martín Espada (poetry)
Earth's hottest day: global average surface temperature more than 2°C (2.06°C) above pre-industrial levels for the first time according to EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service
High profile AI boss Sam Altman sacked from the company he co-founded - OpenAI, the company behind the ChatGPT bot, after the company board "lost confidence" in him
Zimbabwe declares a state of emergency in its capital Harare over a cholera outbreak, with 7,398 suspected cases, 50 confirmed deaths, and 109 people in hospital
23 year old Italian tennis starJannik Sinnerbeats American Taylor Fritz 6-4, 6-4 in Turin to win his first season-ending ATP Finals title
Anne of Brittany becomes engaged to KingCharles VIIIof France
William I of Orangebecomes member of Council of State
The independence of the Church of England is re-established upon ascension ofElizabeth Ito the throne
English explorer, writer and courtierWalter Raleighgoes on trial for treason
Jean Racine's tragedy "Andromaque" premieres in Paris in the private chambers of Queen Marie-Thérèse
Battle of Arcole: French forces underNapoleon Bonapartedefeat an Austrian force led by József Alvinczi after a 3 day battle at Arcole, Republic of Venice
Giuseppe Verdi's opera "Oberto, Conte di San Bonifacio" premieres at the Teatro alla Scala in Milan
David Livingstonebecomes the first European to see Victoria Falls in what is now Zambia and Zimbabwe
Union GeneralAmbrose Burnsidemarches north out of Washington, D.C., to begin the Fredericksburg campaign
Abraham Lincolnbegins first draft of his Gettysburg Address
Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's patriotic Slavonic March makes its premiere in Moscow to a warm reception
W. S. GilbertandArthur Sullivan's comic opera "The Sorcerer" premieres at the Opera Comique, London
Cops arrest boxerJohn L. Sullivanin his 2nd round for being "cruel"
Serial killerH. H. Holmesis arrested in Boston after being tracked there from Philadelphia by the Pinkertons
Vladimir Lenindefends "temporary" removal of freedom of the press
Nikolai Bukharin is expelled from the Soviet Politburo amidst a power struggle withJoseph Stalin
Musical "Sweet & Low" withFanny Bricepremieres in NYC
Charles Lindberghinaugurates Pan Am service from Cuba to South America in the Sikorsky flying boat "American Clipper"
Syracuse fullbackJim Brown, scores NCAA record of 43 pts (vs Colgate)
SF Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerWillie McCoveywins NL Rookie of the Year
US PresidentJFKdedicates Dulles International Airport outside Washington, D.C.
Davy Jonesof the Monkees opens a boutique, Zilch I, in Greenwich Village, New York
BeatleGeorge Harrisonmakes cameo appearance on American television's "The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour"
Douglas Engelbartreceives the patent for the first computer mouse
Elton Johnperforms a live studio concert broadcast for WABC in New York City, later released as his 11-17-70 album
Teri Garrplays new nurse, Lt Suzanne Marquetten, on "The Sniper" episode of TV series M*A*S*H
US PresidentRichard Nixontells AP "...people have got to know whether or not their president is a crook. Well, I'm not a crook"
Swedish pop groupABBAbegins their first international tour at the Kalkonerteater in Copenhagen, Denmark
Egyptian PresidentAnwar Sadatformally accepts invitation to visit Israel
"Salem's Lot", American two-part miniseries based on the horror novel of the same name byStephen King, premieres in the United States
Ayatollah Khomeinifrees most black & female US hostages
Geffen Records releasesJohn LennonandYoko Ono's album "Double Fantasy"
RCA-Victor Records releases "9 to 5 and Odd Jobs", the 23rd studio album by country singer-songwriterDolly Parton
Dale Murphywins NL MVP
Film "Yentl" produced, directed byBarbra Streisand, starring Streisand andMandy Patinkinpremieres, based on play and story by Isaac Bashevis Singer
David Diamond's 9th Symphony, dedicated to Dmitri Mitropoulos, premieres with the American Symphony Orchestra conducted byLeonard Bernstein
TheMadonnacompilation album "You Can Dance" is released
Neil Simon's "Rumors" premieres in NYC
In an all-American finalPete Sampraswins his first of 5 ATP Tour World Championship tennis titles with a 3–6, 7–6, 6–3, 6–4 victory overJim Courierin Frankfurt, Germany
GeneralSani Abachaleads a military coup against Ernest Shonekan's transitional administration and returns the Nigerian government to military control
Mario Lemieuxenters NHL Hall of Fame
Mariah Careyreleases album "#1's"
Whitney Houstonreleases her fourth studio album "My Love is Your Love"
Alberto Fujimoriis removed from office as President of Peru
Australian Leyton Hewitt scores back-to-back season-ending Tennis Masters Cup titles with a classic 7–5, 7–5, 2–6, 2–6, 6–4 victory over SpaniardJuan Carlos Ferreroin Shanghai, China
Britney Spears, at 21 years old, becomes the youngest singer to get a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
Film adaptation of Joanthan Larson's musical "Rent", starringIdina Menzeland Taye Diggs, premieres at Ziegfeld Theater, NYC
"Twilight", based on the book byStephenie Meyer, directed by Catherine Hardwicke, starringKristen StewartandRobert Pattinson, premieres in Los Angeles
German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelwins a record 8th consecutive Formula 1 race with victory in the US Grand Prix at the Circuit of the Americas
ActorCharlie Sheenconfirms that he is HIV-positive
21-year-old Greek tennis star Stefanos Tsitsipas beatsDominic Thiemof Austria 6-7 (6-8), 6-2, 7-6 (7-4) in London to become the youngest winner of the ATP Finals in 18 years
Iran's AyatollahAli Khameneilabels protesters "thugs" after unrest over higher gasoline prices brought Tehran to a standstill day before
Nancy Pelosi, the first female speaker of the US House of Represetnatives and one of its most influential in recent times, announces she will step down
Elizabeth I, aged 25, ascends the English throne upon the death of her half-sister, Queen Mary I
The last sultan of the Ottoman Empire, Mehmed VI, is expelled to Malta on a British warship
Julian 'the Apostate', Roman Emperor (361-63) tried to re-instate paganism, born in Constantinople (d. 363)
Il Bronzino, Italian Florentine painter in Mannerist style (Eleanor de Toledo & her Son), born in Florence (d. 1572)

Ludwig Freiherr von Hammerstein-Equord, German journalist, born in Berlin, Germany (d. 1996)

Camillo Felgen, Luxembourgish baritone pop and schlager singer ("Bonjour les amies / Hello Friends"), lyricist, translator ("I Want to Hold Your Hand / Komm, gib mir deine Hand"; "She Loves You / Sie liebt dich"), and broadcaster, born in Tétange, Luxembourg (d. 2005)
